transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: Thanks for making the time. To start, tell me about your work.

transcript.speaker: P3
transcript.text: I'm a researcher on a enterprise team.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: How does your team collaborate on findings?

transcript.speaker: P3
transcript.text: People comment async, but mentions get buried so feedback gets missed.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: How did connecting your existing tools go?

transcript.speaker: P3
transcript.text: Connecting the data warehouse was fiddly and ate real engineering time.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: How did pricing factor into your decision?

transcript.speaker: P3
transcript.text: The pricing was opaque — I genuinely could not forecast the cost for next quarter.

transcript.speaker: Moderator
transcript.text: That’s really helpful — anything else before we wrap?

transcript.speaker: P3
transcript.text: No, I think that covers the main pain points.

themes.label: Async feedback gets lost
themes.summary: Teammates review and comment asynchronously but mentions and notifications get lost so feedback is missed.

themes.label: Integration setup costs engineering time
themes.summary: Wiring up the warehouse connection needed repeated engineering work before the sync was reliable.

themes.label: Pricing opacity erodes trust before the first invoice
themes.summary: Buyers cannot predict what the product will cost next quarter, so they stop advocating for it internally with their manager and finance.
